rulings.Case Title: HUBERT WONG et al VS. CALLAHAN, MCCUNE & WILLIS, PLC et al

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 23 2015, Line 12, DEFENDANT CALLAHAN, MCCUNE & WILLIS, PLC, PETER CALLAHAN, LEEH DIBELLO, ANNA GEHRIGER MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Denied as moot in light of the ruling on the Demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: HUBERT WONG et al VS. CALLAHAN, MCCUNE & WILLIS, PLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 23 2015, Line 12, DEFENDANT CALLAHAN, MCCUNE & WILLIS, PLC, PETER CALLAHAN, LEEH DIBELLO, ANNA GEHRIGER AMENDED DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the cause of action for breach of attorney-client contract as the Second Amended Complaint still fails to plead facts to allege a breach of contract. The Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the breach of fiduciary duty cause of action to permit Plaintiffs to allege facts showing that the claim is not barred by the statute of limitations. The Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the cause of action for negligence to permit Plaintiffs to allege, if they can, conduct amounting to general, as opposed to professional, negligence. The Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to both the fraud and negligent misrepresentation causes of action. Neither is pled with the requisite specificity, and the defect was not cured after the Court gave leave to amend the First Amended Complaint. The Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to both emotional distress claims because there are no allegations of outrageous conduct and there is no basis for a negligent infliction of emotional distress claim. Finally, the Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the implied equitable indemnity cause of action because it fails to allege facts sufficient to state such a cause of action, and the opposition fails to offer any cure. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: KRISTA ELAINE YERBY VS. MELVIN JOE RUDDY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Corrected Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Form Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents And For An Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 23, 2015, Line 1 DEFENDANT MELVIN RUDDY'S Corrected Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Form Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents And For An Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: IT APPEARING THAT NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED TO DEFENDANT MELVIN J. RUDDY'S MOTION COMPELLING RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND FOR SANCTIONS, THE MOTION IS GRANTED. THE COURT WILL SIGN THE PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: ON-SITE MANAGER INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. AMWINS INSURANCE BROKERS OF CALIFORNIA, LLC, A ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 23 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT AMWINS INSURANCE BROKERS OF CALIFORNIA, LLC, A DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled in its entirety. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges justifiable reliance to support the intentional misrepresentation cause of action. Defendant fails to show that the negligent misrepresentation claim is time barred. First, the 3-year statute of limitations appears to apply because the claim sounds in deceit. Second, even if the court accepted defendant's arguments, it is unclear when the statute of limitations would have begun to run because the complaint does not facially disclose when plaintiff experienced appreciable harm. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CRYSTAL LEI ET AL VS. DEMAS W YAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joint Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Complaint For Malicious Prosecution And Constructive Fraud, Conspire To Defraud, And Fraudulent Transfers.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 23 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANT DEMAS YAN, CHEUK YAN Joint Motion To Strike Complaint For Malicious Prosecution And Constructive Fraud, Conspire To Defraud, And Fraudulent Transfers. Motion denied. Defendants have made a threshold showing that the causes of action against them arise from protected activity due to the nature of the claims-i.e., malicious prosecution. (See Jarrow Formulas, Inc. v. LaMarche (2003) 31 Cal.4th 728, 734 ["section 425.16 potentially may apply to every malicious prosecution action, because every such action arises from an underlying lawsuit, or petition to the judicial branch"].) However, Plaintiffs have produced sufficient evidence to establish a probability of prevailing. (See Lei Decl. and supporting exhibits.) Defendants have not produced any evidence to rebut Plaintiffs' showing, and Defendants' contention that the Complaint fails to state a claim lacks merit, and any asserted deficiency could be cured by amendment. Plaintiffs' request for attorneys' fees and costs is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: GLOBAL STAFFING SERVICES, LLC VS. JACKNYFE, INC D/B/A/ ECHO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 23 2015, Line 11, PLAINTIFF GLOBAL STAFFING SERVICES, LLC APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER. Granted. Plaintiff must post an undertaking in the amount of $10,000 for the writ to issue. (CCP sec. 489.220.) The claim arises out of a contract, is unsecured, and is commercial, as required by CCP sec. 483.010. Additionally, the amount is readily ascertainable. The contract provides that Plaintiff is entitled to 20% of the base salary of the employee hired by Defendant. The term "base salary" is not ambiguous, as it generally means annual salary when used in everyday language. (See People ex rel. Lockyer v. R.J. Reynolds Tobacco Co. (2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 516, 525-26, "The words of a contract are to be understood in their ordinary and popular sense.") Further, Plaintiff submits evidence establishing that the base salary was $160,000. (Declaration of Alex Dulay, par. 5.) Plaintiff has established the probable validity of the claim by a preponderance of the evidence. (See Hobbs v. Weiss (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 76, 80.) The Court is not persuaded by Defendant's argument that the employee left the company shortly after being hired and therefore Defendant is not obligated to pay. The contract required that Defendant notify Plaintiff in writing of the employee's departure. (Declaration of Alex Dulay, Ex. 1.) Defendant has not provided evidence that notice in writing was provided. Lastly, Defendant has not established that Plaintiff is seeking a writ of attachment for an improper purpose. The emails attached to the Declaration of Khristopher Loux are not properly authenticated and may not be considered. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: GLOBAL STAFFING SERVICES, LLC VS. JACKNYFE, INC D/B/A/ ECHO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 23 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT JACKNYFE, INC D/B/A/ ECHO, KHRISTOPHER LOUX, CHRIS SAAD, LEV WALKIN DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled. As to the breach of contract cause of action, the complaint adequately sets forth the relevant terms of the contract. (Complaint, par. 11.) As to the common counts causes of action, the demurrer is overruled because the demurrer to the breach of contract cause of action is overruled. The complaint adequately alleges alter ego. (Complaint, par. 9.) The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: LORON INVESTMENTS VS. MARINA OLSHANSKY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 23 2015, Line 8, DEFENDANT MARINA OLSHANSKY Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint. The matter is taken OFF-CALENDAR PER THE STIPULATION OF THE PARTIES. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: VALENTINE CORPORATION, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. MORRISON PUMP COMPANY, INC., A FLORIDA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or, In The Alternative, Stay The Action Pursuant To C.C.P. 410.30
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 23 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANT MORRISON PUMP COMPANY, INC., Motion To Dismiss Or, In The Alternative, Stay The Action Pursuant To C.C.P. 410.30. The motion is denied. The declaration of Robert Valentine, Jr. provides that Plaintiff never received a copy of Defendant's terms and conditions. There was no meeting of the minds regarding the forum selection clause and it would be unreasonable to enforce a forum selection clause where Plaintiff had no knowledge of it. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
